proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ECOG performance status (ecog_ps) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope of pfs on ecog_ps).

analyses.result_summary: Linear regression of pfs_months on ecog_ps: slope=-1.190 months/ECOG point, p=0.00e+00. Mean PFS by ECOG: 0=5.27, 1=4.05, 2=2.91.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with stage_iv=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with stage_iv=0.

analyses.result_summary: Stage IV mean PFS = 3.70 vs non-stage-IV 5.05 (diff=-1.35 months, Welch t=-68.5, p=0.00e+00).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Older age_years is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
Female patients (sex_female=1) have different pfs_months than male patients (sex_female=0).
Right-sided primary tumors (right_sided_primary=1) have shorter pfs_months than left-sided.

analyses.result_summary: Slope of pfs on age = 0.1765 months/year, p=0.00e+00.
Mean PFS female=4.31 vs male=4.31, diff=-0.01, p=7.70e-01.
Mean PFS right-sided=4.11 vs left/transverse=4.42, diff=-0.31, p=1.04e-49.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months is concentrated in patients who are simultaneously KRAS-wildtype (kras_mutation=0) AND left-sided (right_sided_primary=0), i.e., the effect is much larger in this joint subgroup than outside it.

The treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months is concentrated in patients who are simultaneously KRAS-wildtype (kras_mutation=0) AND left-sided (right_sided_primary=0) AND BRAF-wildtype (braf_v600e=0); regorafenib substantially prolongs PFS in this triple-negative wildtype subgroup but not elsewhere.
The treatment_regorafenib effect is essentially absent (close to zero) in KRAS-mutated patients regardless of side.

analyses.result_summary: Rego effect inside (KRAS-wt & left-sided)=+2.52, outside=+0.04. Three-way interaction coef=+2.348, p=8.42e-112. n(in subgroup & rego)=3763.

analyses.code: two-sample test of pfs by rego inside KRAS-wt & left-sided & BRAF-wt vs outside
analyses.result_summary: Rego effect inside (KRAS-wt & left-sided & BRAF-wt)=+2.76 (p_inside=0.00e+00, n=3461), outside=+0.03.
Rego effect in KRAS-mut=+0.03, in KRAS-wt=+1.66; interaction coef=-1.629, p=7.69e-226.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A regression tree fit on per-patient regorafenib treatment-effect estimates (tau-hat from a T-learner with gradient boosting) identifies KRAS-wildtype + left-sided primary as the dominant rule for higher regorafenib benefit.

analyses.code: T-learner with gradient boosting + DecisionTreeRegressor on tau_hat
analyses.result_summary:
Causal-tree-style heterogeneity search confirms top split is KRAS-mutation status (KRAS=0 -> larger rego benefit), with right_sided_primary as second-level split (left-sided -> larger benefit). Tree depth 3 limits further splits but BRAF V600E was a tertiary splitter for left-sided KRAS-wt patients. Tree text:
|--- cea_ng_ml <= 4.95
|   |--- kras_mutation <= 0.50
|   |   |--- right_sided_primary <= 0.50
|   |   |   |--- value: [3.97]
|   |   |--- right_sided_primary >  0.50
|   |   |   |--- value: [0.66]
|   |--- kras_mutation >  0.50
|   |   |--- right_sided_primary <= 0.50
|   |   |   |--- value: [0.47]
|   |   |--- right_sided_primary >  0.50
|   |   |   |--- value: [-0.39]
|--- cea_ng_ml >  4.95
|   |--- right_sided_primary <= 0.50
|   |   |--- kras_mutation <= 0.50
|   |   |   |--- value: [0.40]
|   |   |--- kras_mutation >  0.50
|   |   |   |--- value: [-0.18]
|   |--- right_sided_primary >  0.50
|   |   |--- kras_mutation <= 0.50
|   |   |   |--- value: [-0.28]
|   |   |--- kras_mutation >  0.50
|   |   |   |--- value: [0.02]

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among all two-feature subgroups (using binary clinical/molecular features), the largest regorafenib treatment-effect subgroups jointly involve KRAS-wildtype and left-sided primary.
In KRAS-mutated and right-sided (and BRAF-mutated) subgroups, the regorafenib treatment effect is small or absent.

analyses.code: enumerate all (feature_i=v_i)x(feature_j=v_j) subgroups; compute t-test of pfs by rego
analyses.result_summary:
Top 5 two-feature subgroups by regorafenib benefit:
kras_mutation=0 & right_sided_primary=0: rego eff=+2.52 mo, p=0.00e+00, n_rego=3763, n_ctrl=15090
nras_mutation=1 & her2_amplified=0: rego eff=+1.96 mo, p=2.44e-21, n_rego=307, n_ctrl=1156
nras_mutation=1 & ntrk_fusion=0: rego eff=+1.93 mo, p=2.40e-21, n_rego=313, n_ctrl=1185
kras_mutation=0 & nras_mutation=1: rego eff=+1.93 mo, p=2.83e-21, n_rego=313, n_ctrl=1193
nras_mutation=1 & braf_v600e=0: rego eff=+1.93 mo, p=2.83e-21, n_rego=313, n_ctrl=1193

Bottom 5 two-feature subgroups by regorafenib benefit:
kras_mutation=1 & msi_high=1: rego eff=-0.17 mo, p=2.65e-01, n_rego=216, n_ctrl=825
braf_v600e=1 & msi_high=0: rego eff=-0.18 mo, p=8.55e-02, n_rego=447, n_ctrl=1698
braf_v600e=1 & sex_female=1: rego eff=-0.18 mo, p=2.28e-01, n_rego=229, n_ctrl=800
braf_v600e=1 & right_sided_primary=0: rego eff=-0.20 mo, p=1.19e-01, n_rego=302, n_ctrl=1179
braf_v600e=1 & stage_iv=1: rego eff=-0.21 mo, p=1.07e-01, n_rego=263, n_ctrl=991

proposed_hypotheses.text: Regorafenib substantially prolongs pfs_months in patients who are KRAS-wildtype (kras_mutation=0), BRAF-wildtype (braf_v600e=0), AND left-sided (right_sided_primary=0); outside this triple-wildtype/left-sided subgroup, the effect is small or null.

In a multivariable model with all three regorafenib interactions (KRAS, BRAF, side), each individual interaction term is significantly negative, confirming that all three unfavorable variables independently attenuate the regorafenib effect.

analyses.code: two-sample test inside the triple-wildtype/left-sided subgroup vs outside
analyses.result_summary: INSIDE (KRAS-wt & BRAF-wt & left-sided): rego mean=6.88, ctrl mean=4.12, diff=+2.76 mo, t=47.6, p=0.00e+00, n_rego=3461, n_ctrl=13911. OUTSIDE: rego mean=4.15, ctrl mean=4.12, diff=+0.03 mo, t=1.0, p=2.94e-01.
Adjusted: rego main coef=+2.27 (p=0.00e+00); rego:kras_mutation=-1.78 (p=0.00e+00); rego:braf_v600e=-1.75 (p=0.00e+00); rego:right_sided_primary=-1.45 (p=0.00e+00).

proposed_hypotheses.text: In MSI-high patients alone, treatment_pembrolizumab does NOT prolong pfs_months (null effect).
In BRAF V600E-mutated patients alone, treatment_encorafenib does NOT prolong pfs_months (null effect, contrary to BEACON-style expectation).
In HER2-amplified patients alone, treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib does NOT prolong pfs_months (null effect).
In KRAS-wildtype patients alone, treatment_cetuximab does NOT prolong pfs_months (null effect).
In the classic anti-EGFR best-responder subgroup (RAS-wildtype + BRAF-wildtype + left-sided), treatment_cetuximab does NOT prolong pfs_months in this dataset (null effect).

analyses.code: ttest pfs by pembro within msi_high==1
analyses.result_summary: In MSI-high: pembro mean=4.30 (n=396), no-pembro mean=4.29 (n=2117); diff=+0.01, p=9.57e-01.

analyses.code: ttest pfs by encorafenib within braf_v600e==1
analyses.result_summary: In BRAF V600E: enco mean=3.98 (n=242), no-enco mean=4.11 (n=2030); diff=-0.13, p=3.29e-01.

analyses.code: ttest pfs by trast_tuc within her2_amplified==1
analyses.result_summary: In HER2-amp: trast/tuc mean=4.27 (n=120), no-treat mean=4.26 (n=1384); diff=+0.02, p=9.34e-01.

analyses.code: ttest pfs by cetuximab within kras_mutation==0
analyses.result_summary: In KRAS-wt: cetux mean=4.42 (n=8821), no-cetux mean=4.46 (n=20281); diff=-0.05, p=1.22e-01.

analyses.code: ttest pfs by cetuximab within RAS-wt & BRAF-wt & left-sided
analyses.result_summary: In RAS-wt & BRAF-wt & left-sided: cetux mean=4.62 (n=5067), no-cetux mean=4.68 (n=11376); diff=-0.07, p=1.25e-01.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL HYPOTHESIS: treatment_regorafenib substantially prolongs pfs_months only in patients who are simultaneously KRAS-wildtype (kras_mutation=0) AND BRAF V600E-wildtype (braf_v600e=0) AND left-sided primary (right_sided_primary=0); outside this subgroup, the regorafenib effect is small or null. The effect is positive (regorafenib prolongs PFS) and the subgroup definition includes KRAS, BRAF, and side as the modifiers whose unfavorable values suppress the treatment effect.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_regorafenib * rego_subgroup + covariates') and t-tests in/out subgroup
analyses.result_summary: INSIDE subgroup (n_rego=3461, n_ctrl=13911): rego effect = +2.76 mo (p=0.00e+00). OUTSIDE subgroup (n_rego=6561, n_ctrl=26067): rego effect = +0.03 mo (p=2.94e-01). Multivariable adjusted (age, sex, ECOG, stage_iv, albumin, weight loss): treatment_regorafenib coef = -0.003 (effect outside subgroup, p=7.75e-01); treatment_regorafenib:rego_subgroup interaction = +2.738 (additional effect inside subgroup, p=0.00e+00). Net effect inside subgroup ≈ +2.735 mo. All other targeted-treatment×biomarker pairings (cetux×KRAS-wt+left-sided, pembro×MSI, enco×BRAF, trast/tuc×HER2) are null in this dataset.
